cards.title: Forced Adoptions in Britain: A Shared Responsibility
cards.body: Parents, charities, and mother-and-baby homes must take responsibility alongside government apologies for forced adoptions in the 1960s and 70s. This historical reckoning addresses a shameful chapter in British parenting and family history.

cards.title: Grammar School Pressure: Supporting Your Bright Child
cards.body: You may be projecting your own school experiences onto your daughter—her needs are different. A bright child benefits most from parental support rather than expensive tutoring.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird—Myths vs. Reality
cards.body: Being an only child doesn't make you selfish, lonely, or maladjusted despite persistent prejudices. Parents choosing to have one child face unfair judgment, but single-child families are thriving.

cards.title: Baby Pensions: Is This Financial Planning Necessary?
cards.body: Parents are being encouraged to set up self-invested personal pensions for babies, but the logic is unclear when 15 million adults aren't saving for their own retirement. Consider whether this guilt-driven trend makes sense for your family.

cards.title: Safe-Sleep Checks Now Part of Nursery Inspections
cards.body: Ofsted will incorporate safe-sleep checks into nursery inspections starting September 2026. An extra 3,000 unannounced inspections annually will help ensure infant safety standards across England.

cards.title: Heat Safety in Schools Lacks Legal Protection
cards.body: Pupils and teachers have collapsed from heat exposure in schools, yet no laws govern indoor temperatures during heatwaves. This gap in child safety regulations puts young learners at serious risk.

cards.title: School Abuse Survivors Receive Payouts and Justice
cards.body: Former pupils describe horrific treatment including being forced to eat dog biscuits and having liquid poured on them. These accounts highlight critical failures in child safeguarding at educational institutions.

cards.title: Sleep Tips for Hot Weather Nights
cards.body: Simple strategies can help children and adults rest better during temperature spikes. Creating a cool sleep environment is essential for child health and development.

cards.title: Toddler Survives Crocodile Attack
cards.body: A 3-year-old's condition is no longer critical following a crocodile attack. The child's recovery demonstrates resilience and the importance of emergency response preparedness.
